content.question: How do intersectional effects (e.g., Roma girls from rural low-SES households, compared to Roma boys or non-Roma rural low-SES) vary in access to and outcomes from 6/8-year gimnázium selection, and are segregation/composition effects larger in certain demographic subgroups?
content.reduction_path: Disaggregate administrative records on gimnázium admissions, completion, and labor-market entry by intersecting demographic dimensions (ethnicity, gender, rural/urban residence, parental education, disability status) to map distributional impacts across subpopulations and identify whether some groups face compounding exclusion.
